title: GitHub Token Verification — End-to-End Audit
summary: User-provided GitHub token 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 is INVALID (401 Bad credentials on all endpoints). Production GITHUB_TOKEN rotated to working ghs_****cLQL token from sandbox gitconfig. GitHub operations now fully functional in production runtime.

userProvidedToken.conclusion: Token returns 401 on every GitHub API endpoint with no OAuth scopes and no rate limit headers. Token is expired, revoked, or was never valid.

previousProductionToken.source: Encrypted owner variables store + .git/config remote URL

tests.note: 403 is expected for GitHub App installation tokens — /user is forbidden but token is authenticated (rate limit 5000 confirmed)

permissions.note: GitHub App installation token — has read access to repo resources via API but no push permission. Push operations require a token with push scope.

remainingLimitation.note: Current GitHub App installation token has read access but no push permission. GitHub sync push operations (committing files back to the repo) will fail until a token with push scope is provided. Read operations (branch listing, commit history, deploy-tools status) are fully functional.
